name: Deep vs Shallow Modules
one_liner: A module is a rectangle: area = functionality, top edge = interface. Deep = big area, thin top edge.
structure: Two rectangles side by side; the deep one is tall with a narrow top edge, the shallow one is flat and wide.
when_to_deploy: a class/endpoint whose interface is nearly as large as its implementation
deciding whether to split or merge modules
reframes_question: Is this interface much simpler than the implementation it hides — or is the module shallow?
related_principle: the best modules are those whose interfaces are much simpler than their implementations

name: Tactical vs Strategic Investment
one_liner: Tactical programming is faster today and slower forever; a small ongoing investment inverts the curve.
structure: Two progress-over-time curves: tactical starts higher then flattens under complexity; strategic dips slightly then overtakes.
when_to_deploy: a codebase slowing under its own complexity
reframes_question: Are you buying speed today by borrowing it from every future change?

grounding.verbatim_probe: Figure 3.1: At the beginning, a tactical approach to program

name: Pass-through Variable to Context Object
one_liner: A variable threaded through a long chain of methods is a red flag; a context object carries it instead.
structure: A call chain where a variable is passed through every layer (a), redrawn with a shared context object (b).
when_to_deploy: a parameter passed through methods that don't use it
reframes_question: Does each layer add abstraction, or just relay a variable it doesn't need?

grounding.verbatim_probe: a variable that is passed down through a long chain of methods

name: Two Ways to Split a Method
one_liner: Split a method by extracting a subtask (b) or by dividing it into two separate methods (c) — subtask first.
structure: A method (a) branching to: extract-a-subtask (b), or divide-into-two (c).
when_to_deploy: a long method doing two things
deciding how to break up a class
a method whose interface is getting complex
reframes_question: Will splitting yield cleaner abstractions — or just more shallow methods to stitch back together?

name: The Three Symptoms of Complexity
one_liner: Change amplification, cognitive load, and unknown unknowns — the three ways complexity shows up.
structure: A three-item checklist you run against any change.
when_to_deploy: naming what makes a codebase hard to work in
a design review with no vocabulary for the pain
reframes_question: Which of the three symptoms is this code causing — and which is worst?

provenance.note: derived, not cited — synthesized from Ch 2, no single figure

related_principle: complexity is anything that makes software hard to understand and modify

name: The Design Red-Flags Checklist
one_liner: Shallow module, information leakage, temporal decomposition, pass-through, repetition, comment-repeats-code.
structure: A skimmable checklist of the book's named red flags.
when_to_deploy: teaching the vocabulary of bad design
reframes_question: Which named red flag is this — and what principle does it violate?

provenance.note: derived, not cited — assembled from red flags across chapters, no single figure
